question: When your cargo tank has baffles, what handling effect do you expect?

answers.text: There will be less front-to-back surge than there is in tanks without baffles.
answers.explanation: Baffles in liquid tanks help to prevent liquids from quickly moving from back to front when braking and front to back on acceleration. Sloshing from side to side is still possible during turns so it is important to understand how the center of gravity can change if turning too quickly on a corner.

answers.text: There will be less side-to-side surge than there is in tanks without baffles.
There will be more low surge than quick surge.

explanation: Baffles in liquid tanks help to prevent liquids from quickly moving from back to front when braking and front to back on acceleration. Sloshing from side to side is still possible during turns so it is important to understand how the center of gravity can change if turning too quickly on a corner.

question: If you are driving a tanker and need to steer quickly to avoid a hazard you:

answers.text: Should not apply the brakes while turning.
answers.explanation: In an emergency situation use the controlled braking or stab braking method while turning to help prevent a rollover.

answers.text: Should wrap your thumb around the steering wheel before starting the steering movement.
Will find that tankers are easier to counter steer than most vehicles.

explanation: In an emergency situation use the controlled braking or stab braking method while turning to help prevent a rollover.

question: Which of these is the best way to take a curve?

answers.text: Slow to a safe speed before entering, then coast through the curve.
Slow to the safe speed before entering, then accelerate slightly through the curve.
answers.explanation: Slow to a speed that will be safe for the weight and center of gravity of your vehicle prior to reaching the curve. Slightly accelerate through the curve. If the vehicle begins to lean away from the curve, stop accelerating since continuing to accelerate could cause a rollover.

answers.text: Maintain speed until you enter, then brake as you drive through the curve.

explanation: Slow to a speed that will be safe for the weight and center of gravity of your vehicle prior to reaching the curve. Slightly accelerate through the curve. If the vehicle begins to lean away from the curve, stop accelerating since continuing to accelerate could cause a rollover.

question: Hauling liquids in tankers requires special care for two reasons. One reason is the ____ center of gravity.

answers.explanation: The center of gravity of liquid tankers changes as the fluids shift from side to side during turns. Drivers of tankers need to understand how the center of gravity increases during turns so that they may avoid a rollover.

explanation: The center of gravity of liquid tankers changes as the fluids shift from side to side during turns. Drivers of tankers need to understand how the center of gravity increases during turns so that they may avoid a rollover.

question: Empty tank vehicles:

answers.text: Require greater stopping distance than full ones.
answers.explanation: An empty tractor-trailer combination takes longer to stop because the springs are unloaded and thus the brakes must be applied slower to avoid locking up the wheels.

answers.text: Have better traction when stopping than full ones.
Need less space ahead than full ones.

explanation: An empty tractor-trailer combination takes longer to stop because the springs are unloaded and thus the brakes must be applied slower to avoid locking up the wheels.

question: An emergency forces you to stop your tanker quickly or crash. You should:

answers.explanation: In an emergency stop situation use the controlled braking or stab braking method to brake so that your vehicle remains straight unless you need to turn.

answers.text: Use light pressure on the brake pedal.

explanation: In an emergency stop situation use the controlled braking or stab braking method to brake so that your vehicle remains straight unless you need to turn.

question: Side-to-side surge can cause:

answers.explanation: The baffles of liquid tankers do not prevent side-to-side surges. Side-to-side surge happens when making a turn. The liquid will rush (surge) in the opposite direction of the turn. This results in the vehicle's center of gravity increasing to its highest point in the middle of the turn. The higher the center of gravity, the easier it is to roll the vehicle or trailer.

explanation: The baffles of liquid tankers do not prevent side-to-side surges. Side-to-side surge happens when making a turn. The liquid will rush (surge) in the opposite direction of the turn. This results in the vehicle's center of gravity increasing to its highest point in the middle of the turn. The higher the center of gravity, the easier it is to roll the vehicle or trailer.

question: You should be extremely cautious when driving smooth bore tanks, especially when you are:

answers.explanation: Smoothbore tanks have no baffles to slow down the surging of liquids front and back when braking, accelerating or cornering. Be very careful when transporting these types of tanks since the cargo is literally shifting and moving around the entire time.

explanation: Smoothbore tanks have no baffles to slow down the surging of liquids front and back when braking, accelerating or cornering. Be very careful when transporting these types of tanks since the cargo is literally shifting and moving around the entire time.

question: On tank trailers, if your drive or trailer wheels begin to skid, your vehicle may:

answers.explanation: When the rear drive wheels of a truck start to slide from excessive braking, the weight of the trailer(s) can begin to slide. The trailer(s) will begin to pivot around the couplings as the trailer(s) attempts to pass the slowed drive wheels resulting in a jackknife.

explanation: When the rear drive wheels of a truck start to slide from excessive braking, the weight of the trailer(s) can begin to slide. The trailer(s) will begin to pivot around the couplings as the trailer(s) attempts to pass the slowed drive wheels resulting in a jackknife.

question: You are driving on a clear night. You must dim your headlights from high to low. You should adjust your speed so that you can stop within:

answers.text: The distance you will travel in the next 15 seconds.
The distance you can see ahead.
answers.explanation: Since visibility will be reduced when the headlights are dimmed, it is recommended to slow down to match your visibility. You should be able to stop within the distance you can see ahead.

explanation: Since visibility will be reduced when the headlights are dimmed, it is recommended to slow down to match your visibility. You should be able to stop within the distance you can see ahead.

question: You should know the outage needed for the liquids you carry because:

answers.text: Tank baffles are not always legal with outage.
Some of the heaviest liquids do not have any.
Some liquids expand more than others when they get warm.
answers.explanation: Outage refers to the amount a fluid expands when it is heated. A cargo tank should never be completely filled because room needs to be reserved for the outage. Be aware of the amount of outage required for the liquid that you are transporting.

explanation: Outage refers to the amount a fluid expands when it is heated. A cargo tank should never be completely filled because room needs to be reserved for the outage. Be aware of the amount of outage required for the liquid that you are transporting.

question: When should the driver of a tanker that has lost its brakes use a truck escape ramp?

answers.explanation: If a truck has lost its service brake while traveling on a downgrade, always use the truck escape ramp to stop the vehicle before a serious accident occurs.

answers.text: Only if the tank is empty.

explanation: If a truck has lost its service brake while traveling on a downgrade, always use the truck escape ramp to stop the vehicle before a serious accident occurs.

question: If an oncoming vehicle drifts into your lane you should:

answers.text: Try to stop rather than turn.
Use no less than one full turn of steering wheel to avoid it.

answers.explanation: Should a vehicle drift into your lane, veer toward the right to avoid an accident and slow as needed.

explanation: Should a vehicle drift into your lane, veer toward the right to avoid an accident and slow as needed.

question: Which of these statements about stopping distance and speed is true?

answers.text: You need about two times as much stopping distance at 40 mph as at 20 mph.
Total stopping distance is how far you will travel after the brakes are applied at speeds over 30 mph.
Wet roads can double stopping distance at any speed.
answers.explanation: Stopping distance always increases during inclement weather. Wet roads can double the stopping distance regardless of speed. Adjust your speed when driving in bad weather.

explanation: Stopping distance always increases during inclement weather. Wet roads can double the stopping distance regardless of speed. Adjust your speed when driving in bad weather.

question: A traffic emergency requires you to escape to the shoulder or roadside. If possible, slow down to ____ mph before using your brakes.

answers.explanation: Commercial trucks are designed to use engine braking to slow down. The service brakes should only be used to supplement engine braking. Under normal conditions, try to slow to 20 mph before applying the service brakes.

explanation: Commercial trucks are designed to use engine braking to slow down. The service brakes should only be used to supplement engine braking. Under normal conditions, try to slow to 20 mph before applying the service brakes.

question: When you load the small tanks or a cargo tank equipped with bulkheads, you should check your:

answers.explanation: Bulkheads divide up a liquid tank into smaller internal tanks. It is important to be aware of weight distribution when loading and unloading this type of tank.

explanation: Bulkheads divide up a liquid tank into smaller internal tanks. It is important to be aware of weight distribution when loading and unloading this type of tank.

question: Baffles in liquid cargo tanks do not usually prevent ____ surge.

answers.explanation: Surge happens as fluids shift in a tanker. Baffles in a tanker help prevent surges to the front and to the back, but not side-to-side surges. Side-to-side surges happen when cornering. As you turn, the fluids will surge in the opposite direction of the turn and raise the center of gravity, which may result in a rollover.

explanation: Surge happens as fluids shift in a tanker. Baffles in a tanker help prevent surges to the front and to the back, but not side-to-side surges. Side-to-side surges happen when cornering. As you turn, the fluids will surge in the opposite direction of the turn and raise the center of gravity, which may result in a rollover.

question: The best way to take a curve is to slow to a safe speed before entering the curve, then ____ as you go through it.

answers.explanation: Accelerating slowly through the curve will give the driver greater control during the turn. However, always be aware of a possible rollover condition and stop accelerating if the vehicle begins to lean away from the direction of the turn.

explanation: Accelerating slowly through the curve will give the driver greater control during the turn. However, always be aware of a possible rollover condition and stop accelerating if the vehicle begins to lean away from the direction of the turn.

question: You are driving on a clear night with your low beam lights on. If your headlights let you see about 250 feet ahead of you, you should adjust your speed so that you can stop within about ____ feet.

answers.explanation: Since visibility will be reduced when the headlights are dimmed, it is recommended to slow down to match your visibility. You should be able to stop within the distance of visibility, in this case, 250 feet.

explanation: Since visibility will be reduced when the headlights are dimmed, it is recommended to slow down to match your visibility. You should be able to stop within the distance of visibility, in this case, 250 feet.

question: Which of these statements about emergency steering is true?

answers.text: You can almost always stop more quickly than you can turn.
Quick steering movements should only be done with the wheels locked.
If an oncoming driver drifts into your lane, a move to your right may be best.
answers.explanation: Should a vehicle drift into your lane, veer toward the right to avoid an accident and slow as needed.

explanation: Should a vehicle drift into your lane, veer toward the right to avoid an accident and slow as needed.

question: Hauling liquids in tank vehicles requires special care for two reasons. One reason is:

answers.explanation: Tanker truck drivers need to understand how liquid movement changes the center of gravity of the vehicle during turns, potentially resulting in a rollover.

explanation: Tanker truck drivers need to understand how liquid movement changes the center of gravity of the vehicle during turns, potentially resulting in a rollover.

question: How would you expect a truck with a baffled cargo tank to handle on the road?

answers.text: There will be less forward and backward liquid surge than there is in tanks without baffles.
answers.explanation: Since baffles help reduce forward surge during braking and rearward surge during acceleration, the driver of a baffled tank should notice less surge when stopping or accelerating. However, baffles do not reduce side-to-side surge. Both a baffled tank and a tank without baffles will have the same side-to-side surge issues during turns and will need to slow down around corners to avoid rolling over.

answers.text: The truck will handle the same as a tanker without baffles.
The truck will seem heavier than it really is.

explanation: Since baffles help reduce forward surge during braking and rearward surge during acceleration, the driver of a baffled tank should notice less surge when stopping or accelerating. However, baffles do not reduce side-to-side surge. Both a baffled tank and a tank without baffles will have the same side-to-side surge issues during turns and will need to slow down around corners to avoid rolling over.

question: Except when you are loading or unloading cargo, the tank liquid discharge valve should always be:

answers.explanation: It is a violation to move a vehicle containing any amount of hazardous materials with valves or covers opened unless it is completely empty. After loading a tanker, verify that all valves are completely closed and that any manhole covers are back in position prior to moving the vehicle.

explanation: It is a violation to move a vehicle containing any amount of hazardous materials with valves or covers opened unless it is completely empty. After loading a tanker, verify that all valves are completely closed and that any manhole covers are back in position prior to moving the vehicle.

question: Which of the statements about tank vehicles is true?

answers.text: Fully loaded tank vehicles take longer to stop than empty ones.
You should use only the emergency brakes to stop a tank vehicle in order to avoid a crash.
The posted speed for a curve may be too fast for a tank vehicle.
answers.explanation: The recommended speeds posted for curves are intended for light trucks and passenger cars. Commercial trucks will need to drive slower and since tankers have the additional issue of side-to-side surge they will need to drive the slowest to avoid causing a rollover.

explanation: The recommended speeds posted for curves are intended for light trucks and passenger cars. Commercial trucks will need to drive slower and since tankers have the additional issue of side-to-side surge they will need to drive the slowest to avoid causing a rollover.

question: When transporting a tanker, you should inspect the cargo within the first ____ miles of the trip.

answers.explanation: When transporting cargo a driver must stop to inspect that the cargo is secured within the first 50 miles and then every 150 miles or 3 hours, whichever occurs first.

explanation: When transporting cargo a driver must stop to inspect that the cargo is secured within the first 50 miles and then every 150 miles or 3 hours, whichever occurs first.

question: Before loading or unloading the tanker, the first thing you should do is:

answers.text: Make sure you have all the necessary plans.
Properly secure all of the baffles.

answers.explanation: Prior to loading or unloading a tanker, the driver needs to perform a walk around inspection of the vehicle and load site.

explanation: Prior to loading or unloading a tanker, the driver needs to perform a walk around inspection of the vehicle and load site.

question: When inspecting the pipe connections and hoses for leaks, pay special attention to:

answers.explanation: The most likely area for a leak to occur will be the joint connections between pipes and hoses.

explanation: The most likely area for a leak to occur will be the joint connections between pipes and hoses.

question: Portable tanks are;

answers.text: Can only be loaded when they are attached to the vehicle.
Not allowed to carry loads that are more than 1,000 gallons.
Bulk containers, which are not permanently attached to the truck.
answers.explanation: Portable tanks are not permanently attached to a vehicle. These bulk containers are loaded and unloaded while the tanks are off the vehicle before the tanks are placed on the transport.

explanation: Portable tanks are not permanently attached to a vehicle. These bulk containers are loaded and unloaded while the tanks are off the vehicle before the tanks are placed on the transport.

question: Vehicles skids are often caused by:

answers.explanation: Driving too fast for conditions is the main cause of skids. Always slow down when conditions are not ideal.

answers.text: Loose materials on a road surface.

explanation: Driving too fast for conditions is the main cause of skids. Always slow down when conditions are not ideal.

question: If you steer a tank vehicle too quickly while braking you:

answers.text: May cause vehicle to roll over.
answers.explanation: Steering a tanker while braking will significant effect the center of gravity of a load increasing the likelihood of a rollover.

answers.text: Can reduce the amount of liquid surge.

explanation: Steering a tanker while braking will significant effect the center of gravity of a load increasing the likelihood of a rollover.

question: Which of the statements about emergency steering and tankers is true?

answers.text: When making quick steering movements, do not apply the brakes.
answers.explanation: When steering to avoid an accident do not apply the brakes. Turn just enough to avoid a collision, and be ready to countersteer after clearing the obstacle.

answers.text: A tanker is easier to steer than most other vehicles.
The clutch should be depressed before making a quick steering movement.

explanation: When steering to avoid an accident do not apply the brakes. Turn just enough to avoid a collision, and be ready to countersteer after clearing the obstacle.

question: Baffled liquid tanks have bulkheads:

answers.text: With holes in them that let liquid flow through them.
answers.explanation: Baffled tanks slow the flow of fluids but do not completely block it. Holes in the bulkheads allow fluids to travel between the bulkheads, but at a much slower rate than if there were no bulkheads. The bulkheads slow movement from front to back and back to front but do not affect side-to-side movements.

answers.text: That increase the amount of side-to-side surges.
That separate the liquids from different areas of the tanks.

explanation: Baffled tanks slow the flow of fluids but do not completely block it. Holes in the bulkheads allow fluids to travel between the bulkheads, but at a much slower rate than if there were no bulkheads. The bulkheads slow movement from front to back and back to front but do not affect side-to-side movements.

question: Tanks transporting food products are required to be unbaffled because of:

answers.text: The ease in cleaning the outside of the tank.
The difficulty in cleaning the inside of the tank.
answers.explanation: Baffles make it difficult to sanitize a tanker. Therefore, food products such as milk trucks will be unbaffled. Baffling will be used on tankers that haul gasoline and products that do not require sanitation.

explanation: Baffles make it difficult to sanitize a tanker. Therefore, food products such as milk trucks will be unbaffled. Baffling will be used on tankers that haul gasoline and products that do not require sanitation.

question: Forward-to-back surge on a slippery surface can:

answers.text: Shove a stopped truck into an intersection.
answers.explanation: Assuming that the tanker does not have baffles, a forward-to-back surge will cause a wave that hits the back of the tank, then the front of the tank, and repeats until the wave dissipates. The wave hitting the front of the tank can push the truck into an intersection during slippery conditions if the driver doesn't allow for enough time and distance to stop safely.

explanation: Assuming that the tanker does not have baffles, a forward-to-back surge will cause a wave that hits the back of the tank, then the front of the tank, and repeats until the wave dissipates. The wave hitting the front of the tank can push the truck into an intersection during slippery conditions if the driver doesn't allow for enough time and distance to stop safely.

question: What does liquid surge do to the handling of a tanker?

answers.text: It can move the truck in the direction the liquid waves move.
answers.explanation: Liquid surge has negative effects on a tanker's handling characteristics. The waves within the tanker can cause the vehicle to move in the direction of the wave. For example, hitting the brakes too hard will initially surge the fluid forward and increased pressure from the forward surge will require additional braking distance to stop.

answers.text: Surge raises the wind drag of the truck.

explanation: Liquid surge has negative effects on a tanker's handling characteristics. The waves within the tanker can cause the vehicle to move in the direction of the wave. For example, hitting the brakes too hard will initially surge the fluid forward and increased pressure from the forward surge will require additional braking distance to stop.

question: Factors which determine the amount of liquid that may be loaded into a cargo tank include all the following, EXCEPT:

answers.explanation: Factors that limit the amount of fluid that may be loaded onto a cargo tanker include the weight limits of the vehicle or substance (hazardous materials limits) and the anticipated amount of fluid outage.

explanation: Factors that limit the amount of fluid that may be loaded onto a cargo tanker include the weight limits of the vehicle or substance (hazardous materials limits) and the anticipated amount of fluid outage.

question: Which of the following properly describes a baffled tanker?

answers.text: Having bulkheads with holes that slow down fluids moving front and rearwards.
answers.explanation: A baffled tanker has bulkheads with holes that slow down fluids moving front and rearwards.

answers.text: Having bulkheads with holes that slow down fluids moving side to side.
Bulkheads that separate and isolate fluids so they cannot move around as much.

explanation: A baffled tanker has bulkheads with holes that slow down fluids moving front and rearwards.

question: Which technique should be used to stop as soon as possible to avoid an accident?

answers.text: Brake as hard as possible and hold the steering wheel straight.
Apply the emergency brake and the foot brake until stopped.

answers.explanation: Both stab braking and controlled braking will allow the vehicle to stop while maintaining control of the vehicle. Allowing the vehicle to jackknife or rollover by keeping the brakes locked will complicate the issue.

explanation: Both stab braking and controlled braking will allow the vehicle to stop while maintaining control of the vehicle. Allowing the vehicle to jackknife or rollover by keeping the brakes locked will complicate the issue.

question: Each of the following is a concern for smooth bore tankers, EXCEPT:

answers.text: Braking hard to stop at an intersection

answers.explanation: Lacking baffles, the liquids of smoothbore tanks can shift directions quickly making them more prone to roll on curves or push the vehicle forward during hard braking.

answers.text: Carrying to much speed into a corner.

explanation: Lacking baffles, the liquids of smoothbore tanks can shift directions quickly making them more prone to roll on curves or push the vehicle forward during hard braking.

question: When transporting a tanker, once you slow prior to entering a curve, you should then:

answers.explanation: When cornering, you should slow to the desired safe speed before the corner, and then slightly accelerate through the corner.

answers.text: downshift to the next lower gear

explanation: When cornering, you should slow to the desired safe speed before the corner, and then slightly accelerate through the corner.

question: Which characteristic is most associated with a smooth bore tank?

answers.explanation: A smooth bore tank lacks any baffles, or bulkheads, to slow the movement of fluids during transport.

explanation: A smooth bore tank lacks any baffles, or bulkheads, to slow the movement of fluids during transport.

question: A commercial motor vehicle that transports a bulk cargo of liquid or gas within one or more tanks that are either permanently or temporarily attached will primarily require a:

answers.explanation: The tank endorsement (N) will be primarily required for transporting tanks. Other endorsements such as hazardous materials or double/triples are additional endorsements that may be required for particular situations.

explanation: The tank endorsement (N) will be primarily required for transporting tanks. Other endorsements such as hazardous materials or double/triples are additional endorsements that may be required for particular situations.

question: Which term is used to describe the movement of liquids in partially filled tanks?

answers.explanation: Surge refers to liquid movements in partially filled cargo tanks.

explanation: Surge refers to liquid movements in partially filled cargo tanks.

question: Each of the following statements concerning cargo tankers is true, EXCEPT:

answers.text: Surge can push a tractor forward when trying to stop
They can rollover easier than other types of trailers.
They have a lower center of gravity than non-tankers
answers.explanation: Due to surge and their high center of gravity, tankers are more prone to roll over and push the tractor forward during hard braking.

explanation: Due to surge and their high center of gravity, tankers are more prone to roll over and push the tractor forward during hard braking.

question: Which term refers to the expansion or contraction of a liquid due to changes in temperature?

answers.explanation: Liquids will expand and contract based on the temperature. Different liquids expand and contract at different rates. The space reserved to handle expansion is called outage.

explanation: Liquids will expand and contract based on the temperature. Different liquids expand and contract at different rates. The space reserved to handle expansion is called outage.

question: What are the proper inspection intervals for a cargo tanker vehicle?

answers.text: Before loading and after loading only.
Pre-trip, before loading, after unloading, and post-trip inspections.
answers.explanation: The vehicle should be inspected during pre and post-trip, along with inspections before loading and after unloading.

explanation: The vehicle should be inspected during pre and post-trip, along with inspections before loading and after unloading.

question: Which of the following BEST describes a high center of gravity?

answers.text: The height of a trailer, regardless of load, higher than an average trailer.
The height of only the load, is stacked higher than normal on a vehicle.
The center of the mass of the vehicle and load together is higher off the ground than the average vehicle.
answers.explanation: The center of the mass of any object is the center of gravity. This center includes the entire vehicle, the load, the fuel in the tractor, and the driver combined. When this center is higher than the average vehicle it has a high center of gravity making it more prone to roll over. Loaded tankers have a high center of gravity compared to the average tractor-trailer.

explanation: The center of the mass of any object is the center of gravity. This center includes the entire vehicle, the load, the fuel in the tractor, and the driver combined. When this center is higher than the average vehicle it has a high center of gravity making it more prone to roll over. Loaded tankers have a high center of gravity compared to the average tractor-trailer.

question: Where should the weight be distributed when filling a tanker with bulkheads?

answers.text: Evenly across the length of the tanker
answers.explanation: If a tanker has bulkheads to help separate the load in the tank, distribute the load evenly so that neither the front nor rear of the tank has more weight than the other.

explanation: If a tanker has bulkheads to help separate the load in the tank, distribute the load evenly so that neither the front nor rear of the tank has more weight than the other.

question: Which term is used to describe components that separate liquids in the cargo tanks and provide holes to allow liquids to slowly flow front or back?

answers.explanation: Baffles placed in bulkheads allow fluids to transfer front and back at a much slower rate than if they were not present.

explanation: Baffles placed in bulkheads allow fluids to transfer front and back at a much slower rate than if they were not present.

question: Which of the following should primarily be used for your inspection process for your cargo tanker?

answers.explanation: Cargo tanker designs are varied. Always refer to the operator's manual for your particular vehicle. The CDL handbook can only provide general information that is applicable to most tankers.

answers.text: Your experience with other types of cargo tankers.

explanation: Cargo tanker designs are varied. Always refer to the operator's manual for your particular vehicle. The CDL handbook can only provide general information that is applicable to most tankers.

question: Which commodity would require the use of an un-baffled tanker over a baffled one?

answers.explanation: Although baffled tankers provide some extra stability from surging, it is difficult to keep them clean enough to transport food. Dairy milk requires an unbaffled tanker because they are easier to clean and sanitize.

explanation: Although baffled tankers provide some extra stability from surging, it is difficult to keep them clean enough to transport food. Dairy milk requires an unbaffled tanker because they are easier to clean and sanitize.

question: Regarding a cargo tanker, which item would be most critical to inspect?

answers.explanation: It is illegal to transport liquids if they are leaking from the tank. It could also cause hazardous materials and environmental issues. Ensuring that there are no leaks on the tanker is critical.

explanation: It is illegal to transport liquids if they are leaking from the tank. It could also cause hazardous materials and environmental issues. Ensuring that there are no leaks on the tanker is critical.

question: What method of braking should be used to control surge?

answers.text: Braking early to maximize the amount of time to slow down
answers.explanation: You should brake early to allow the most time to brake and slow down. Hold the brake pedal steady until stopped.

explanation: You should brake early to allow the most time to brake and slow down. Hold the brake pedal steady until stopped.

question: Reasons for not completely filling a cargo tank include all the following, EXCEPT:

answers.explanation: You will need to allow for outage while staying below any weight limits. Freezing is not likely to be an issue for the types and volumes of fluids being transported.

explanation: You will need to allow for outage while staying below any weight limits. Freezing is not likely to be an issue for the types and volumes of fluids being transported.

question: Which of the following does NOT effect the amount of liquid expansion of a material being transported?

answers.explanation: The type of liquid will determine the rate of thermal expansion, the expected temperature will determine the amount of thermal expansion.

explanation: The type of liquid will determine the rate of thermal expansion, the expected temperature will determine the amount of thermal expansion.

question: Liquid surge will be most concerning when braking in which situation?

answers.explanation: When braking the forward surge can push the tractor forward making icy and snowy intersections especially dangerous. Give yourself plenty of room to slow down and stop.

explanation: When braking the forward surge can push the tractor forward making icy and snowy intersections especially dangerous. Give yourself plenty of room to slow down and stop.

question: Willfully driving a leaking cargo tanker may result in:

answers.text: A citation, loss of driving privileges, and paying for the cleanup.
answers.explanation: A driver that willfully continues with a leaking cargo tanker may receive a citation, loss of driving privileges, and be required to pay the cost of cleanup.

explanation: A driver that willfully continues with a leaking cargo tanker may receive a citation, loss of driving privileges, and be required to pay the cost of cleanup.

question: Which combination of endorsements is required to transport hazardous materials in a tanker?

answers.explanation: The driver will require a hazardous materials endorsement (H) and tank vehicle (N) endorsement

explanation: The driver will require a hazardous materials endorsement (H) and tank vehicle (N) endorsement

question: At which fluid capacity does it become necessary to have a tank vehicle endorsement?

answers.text: When one tank has a capacity greater than 119 gallons, or multiple tanks exceed 1,000 gallons
answers.explanation: A tank vehicle (N) endorsement will be required when one tank has a capacity greater than 119 gallons, or multiple tanks exceed 1,000 gallons.

answers.text: When one tank has a capacity greater than 99 gallons, or multiple tanks exceed 700 gallons
When one tank has a capacity greater than 149 gallons, or multiple tanks exceed 1,500 gallons

explanation: A tank vehicle (N) endorsement will be required when one tank has a capacity greater than 119 gallons, or multiple tanks exceed 1,000 gallons.

question: Which part is used to section a cargo tanker into individual compartments that do not mix together?

answers.explanation: Bulkheads that do not contain any baffles will compartmentalize the cargo tanker into sections of liquids that do not mix together, helping to resist surging.

explanation: Bulkheads that do not contain any baffles will compartmentalize the cargo tanker into sections of liquids that do not mix together, helping to resist surging.

question: When may a HazMat vehicle be moved with open manhole covers or vents?

answers.text: Only when the tank is completely empty
answers.explanation: In practice, never move a tanker with an open manhole cover or vent. Technically, you may move a tanker that is completely empty with either open.

answers.text: Never, even when empty fumes still remain

explanation: In practice, never move a tanker with an open manhole cover or vent. Technically, you may move a tanker that is completely empty with either open.

question: Which of the following is associated with the need to press the brake pedal harder and harder to slow or maintain control on a downgrade?

answers.explanation: Brake fade will first start to be noticed as you find it necessary to apply the brake pedal harder each time you press it, to continue slowing or controlling a descent on a downgrade.

explanation: Brake fade will first start to be noticed as you find it necessary to apply the brake pedal harder each time you press it, to continue slowing or controlling a descent on a downgrade.

question: When driving a cargo tanker, how far do you need to stop before a railroad crossing?

answers.text: Within 5 to 40 feet of the nearest rail.
Within 25 to 60 feet of the nearest rail.
Within 15 to 50 feet of the nearest rail.
answers.explanation: As with other types of CMVs, you must stop within 15 to 50 feet of the nearest rail when approaching a railroad crossing.

explanation: As with other types of CMVs, you must stop within 15 to 50 feet of the nearest rail when approaching a railroad crossing.

question: What is the purpose of draining air tanks?

answers.text: To prevent compressor oil from building up.
To prevent water built up from freezing.
answers.explanation: Compressing air causes condensation that builds up within the system. If this water freezes it can disrupt proper air brake operation. Draining the water out each evening will help prevent this from happening.

answers.text: To release air pressure prior to storage.

explanation: Compressing air causes condensation that builds up within the system. If this water freezes it can disrupt proper air brake operation. Draining the water out each evening will help prevent this from happening.

question: How close to a cargo tanker must a qualified person be while overseeing the loading or unloading of the vehicle?

answers.explanation: A qualified person overseeing the loading or unloading must be within 25 feet of the operation

explanation: A qualified person overseeing the loading or unloading must be within 25 feet of the operation

question: Regarding hydraulic brakes, which of the following is NOT a recommended reaction when it appears the brakes are starting to fail?

answers.text: Begin upshifting to start engine braking
answers.explanation: Pump the brake pedal to build pressure. If that does not work, begin downshifting to use engine braking and slowly apply the emergency brake to stop.

answers.text: Pump the brake pedal in an attempt to build pressure
Slowly apply the emergency brake to stop

explanation: Pump the brake pedal to build pressure. If that does not work, begin downshifting to use engine braking and slowly apply the emergency brake to stop.

question: When starting a vehicle on a slight grade, how should you prevent the vehicle from rolling?

answers.text: Increase engine RPM, then completely release the clutch and brake pedal simultaneously
Release the parking brake after you start moving
Release the clutch until you feel it engage before releasing the brake
answers.explanation: Release the clutch until you feel it engage, then release the brake pedal, and accelerate as you continue to release the clutch.

explanation: Release the clutch until you feel it engage, then release the brake pedal, and accelerate as you continue to release the clutch.

question: Which of the following is recommended if you must make a quick steering movement to avoid an accident?

answers.text: Try to avoid braking while you are steering
answers.explanation: Try to avoid braking and making quick turns at the same time to reduce the amount of movement by the liquid in the tanker. Turning and braking together will compound the pressure created by the liquid.

answers.text: Brake when you begin to countersteer
Let the truck straighten out in the new direction before turning back to your lane

explanation: Try to avoid braking and making quick turns at the same time to reduce the amount of movement by the liquid in the tanker. Turning and braking together will compound the pressure created by the liquid.
